title: Regular Expressions (RegEx)
content:
### Regular Expressions

**RegEx** is a sequence of characters that defines a search pattern.

---

### The `re` Module

```python
import re
```

---

### `re.findall()` -- Find All Matches

```python
import re

text = "The rain in Spain"
result = re.findall("ai", text)
print(result)  # ["ai", "ai"]
```

---

### `re.search()` -- First Match

```python
import re

text = "The rain in Spain"
result = re.search("\\s", text)  # First whitespace

if result:
    print(result.start())  # 3 (position)
```

---

### `re.split()` -- Split by Pattern

```python
import re

text = "The rain in Spain"
result = re.split("\\s", text)
print(result)  # ["The", "rain", "in", "Spain"]
```

---

### `re.sub()` -- Replace

```python
import re

text = "The rain in Spain"
result = re.sub("\\s", "-", text)
print(result)  # "The-rain-in-Spain"
```

---

### Common Patterns

| Pattern | Matches |
|---------|---------|
| `\\d` | Digit (0-9) |
| `\\w` | Word character (a-z, A-Z, 0-9, _) |
| `\\s` | Whitespace (space, tab, newline) |
| `[abc]` | Any character in brackets |
| `[^abc]` | Any character NOT in brackets |
| `^` | Start of string |
| `$` | End of string |
| `.` | Any character (except newline) |
| `*` | Zero or more |
| `+` | One or more |
| `?` | Zero or one |
| `{n}` | Exactly n times |
| `{n,}` | n or more times |

---

### Email Validation Example

```python
import re

def is_valid_email(email):
    pattern = r"^[\w\.-]+@[\w\.-]+\.\w+$"
    return bool(re.match(pattern, email))

print(is_valid_email("test@example.com"))  # True
print(is_valid_email("test@com"))          # False
```

> **Tip:** Use raw strings `r"..."` for regex patterns to avoid escaping backslashes.
codeExample:
import re

text = "Call 555-1234 or 555-5678"

# Find all phone numbers
numbers = re.findall(r"\d{3}-\d{4}", text)
print("Phone numbers:", numbers)

# Replace
expanded = re.sub(r"\d{3}-\d{4}", "[NUMBER]", text)
print("Masked:", expanded)

# Check pattern
email = "user@example.com"
pattern = r"^[\w\.-]+@[\w\.-]+\.\w+$"
if re.match(pattern, email):
    print(f"{email} is valid")

quiz.question: Which function finds all matches of a pattern?
